title: The Cavalry of Rome
designIntent: DSK-composed WebGPU-first fidelity route with no in-game UI DOM or loading overlay UI: scan over a larger pannable realistic Roman terrain map, hover oversized highlighted regions, click to trigger a cinematic dive, then enter a fixed-aligned Rome-perspective hex battlefield with aggregated troop-class units, terrain tiles, and terrain-grounded battlefield features.
standingIterationRule: Every future gameplay iteration should improve visual fidelity as a secondary goal and should not regress terrain density, vegetation descriptors, lighting mood, region readability, soldier fidelity, hex readability, or cinematic composition.

currentGameplayStep.intent: After region selection, cover the old freeform battlefield with a no-UI hex battlefield viewed from above and behind Rome, using aligned terrain hexes and aggregated troop pieces.
currentGameplayStep.implementation: hex-battlefield-pass.js creates an 11x9 Rome-perspective hex grid, grass/water/hill/fence tiles, aggregated light/medium/heavy units, class body colors, army band colors, direct hex hover, and Rome-only unit hover/selection. The hex math now uses fixed pointy-offset spacing with a single radius and y-scale so hexes no longer drift in alignment or size by row. The old screen-space vegetation drawing is disabled to stop sky-floating plants and reduce panning/selection jank.

fidelityFocus.terrain: realistic low-poly terrain with domain warping, FBM/ridged noise, blended biome colors, moisture/slope/river masks, and non-repeating landforms
fidelityFocus.mapNavigation: large pannable region-selection surface with keyboard/drag pan and wheel zoom
fidelityFocus.regions: oversized highlighted region meshes with glow, wobble borders, and DSK affordance descriptors

hexBattlefield.camera: above and behind Rome, looking across the whole tactical field
hexBattlefield.grid: 11x9 fixed pointy-offset hex board with consistent radius/y-scale covering the old freeform battlefield
hexBattlefield.terrain: grass, water, hill, and fence hexes with movement/defense metadata
hexBattlefield.units: aggregated troop markers representing groups of soldiers rather than individual bodies
hexBattlefield.selection: only Rome-side units are hover-highlighted and selectable
hexBattlefield.colors: light troops green, medium troops blue, heavy troops red; army ownership appears as a colored band
hexBattlefield.enemyBands: region-based gold, white, purple, or black bands

fidelityFocus.uiDom: none; runtime status hooks are non-DOM sinks only
fidelityFocus.loaderUi: none; shared route page loader is intentionally omitted for this game endpoint

proceduralVegetation.grass: deterministic grass descriptor field is retained as metadata for future terrain-anchored instancing
proceduralVegetation.screenSpaceRendering: disabled because it was visually floating in the sky and caused panning/selection jank
proceduralVegetation.battlefieldGrounding: tile features draw directly on the hex playfield so they cannot detach from terrain
proceduralVegetation.reeds: river and moisture-driven reed descriptors retained for future grounded rendering
proceduralVegetation.shrubs: slope/moisture-aware shrub and scrub descriptors retained for future grounded rendering
proceduralVegetation.trees: biome-aware primitive grove descriptors retained for future grounded rendering
proceduralVegetation.flowersAndRocks: small wildflower and rock descriptors retained for future grounded rendering
proceduralVegetation.boundary: local procedural vegetation descriptor DSK candidate owns placements; no screen-space overlay is rendered until true terrain-anchored WebGPU instancing exists

fidelityFocus.soldiers: full-bodied primitive soldiers remain in the underlying cinematic renderer, but tactical hex mode uses aggregated unit markers with small soldier clusters for readability
fidelityFocus.battlefield: aligned hex grid, terrain tiles, aggregated troop pieces, region terrain variation, and no visible UI

protectedExpressionGuardrails: Do not copy tabletop scenario layouts, card systems, dice faces, command sections, rule text, or unit-stat tables.
Keep the route visual-first until the cinematic terrain and battlefield proof is stable.
Use Roman-history flavor as original visual composition, not as a digital recreation of a board-game ruleset.
currentLocalSurfaces: realistic terrain mesh color and brush-stroke overlay generation
large pannable region ellipse placement and labels
procedural vegetation descriptor generation without screen-space presentation
aggregated troop unit formation and rendering
cinematic camera interpolation and map pan/zoom controls
non-DOM runtime sink shim for status/readout compatibility

rendererOwnership.hexBattlefieldOverlay: scene-native tactical presentation and first gameplay interaction layer
dskOwned: procedural vegetation descriptor snapshot via local DSK candidate
hex battlefield snapshot via local DSK candidate

nextLedge: Add scene-native unit movement on the aligned hex board with terrain movement costs, preserving no UI DOM and improving battlefield visual fidelity.
